segments.text: Japan's attention to detail is insane.
And these designs will prove it. You see, public toilets are terrifying. You don't know if it's clean. You don't know if somebody's inside, so you hesitate.
But one architecture in Japan said enough. And he introduced transparent toilets in Tokyo. Clear glass, so you see everything before entering. And once
you lock the door, everything will turn opaque. Total privacy instantly. And result, no hesitation, no guessing.
Smart. But the next one fixes something which you never questioned. See, your fridge opens one way. If kitchen layout is wrong, you're stuck with it. But what
if it works both ways? So, they have this dual swing door. Left, right, same fridge and no compromise. Small places,
left-handers, guest, all the problems are solved by one go. Innovative. But next one you experience on a daily basis. It's butter. Cold hard and it
rips your toes. And even if you use a knife, it's difficult. Then some Japanese designers saw a glue stick and then wondered why not butter. So, you finally done it. Now you can twist,
spread and done. No knife, no cleanup, no friction.
